sanskrit: आहुस्त्वामृषयः सर्वे देवर्षिर्नारदस्तथा । असितो देवलो व्यासः स्वयं चैव ब्रवीषि मे ॥
hindiTranslation: अर्जुन बोले  परम ब्रह्म, परम धाम और महान् पवित्र आप ही हैं ।  आप शाश्वत, दिव्य पुरुष, आदिदेव, अजन्मा और विभु व्यापक हैं  ऐसा सबकेसब ऋषि, देवर्षि नारद, असित, देवल तथा व्यास कहते हैं और स्वयं आप भी मेरे प्रति कहते हैं ॥
englishTranslation: All the sages speak of you this way: the divine sage Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa. You yourself also say this to me.
essence: The deepest recognition is echoed by the wise and by Krishna himself.
speaker: Arjuna
simpleMeaning: Arjuna says that all the great sages, including Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa, speak of Krishna this way, and Krishna himself says the same to him.
simpleInsight: When every trusted voice agrees, doubt begins to soften.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield of Kurukshetra, Arjuna has begun to see Krishna not just as a charioteer, but as the supreme being. He now points to the authority of sages like Narada, Asita, Devala, and Vyasa to confirm what Krishna has already revealed to him.
modernRelevance: You keep doubting a hard truth until the people you trust most all say the same thing. Then the resistance weakens, and you can finally listen.
translationLiteral: आहुः (they say) / त्वाम् (you) / ऋषयः (the sages) / सर्वे (all) / देवर्षिः (the divine sage) / नारदः (Narada) / तथा (and also) / असितः (Asita) / देवलः (Devala) / व्यासः (Vyasa) / स्वयम् (yourself) / च (and) / एव (indeed) / ब्रवीषि (you speak) / मे (to me)
insight: It shifts faith from private doubt to confirmed recognition through respected testimony.
